EducationMarch 2, 2026

CGC Label Colors Explained

Understanding CGC label colors is one of the most important skills any comic collector can develop. Blue, Purple, Green, Yellow — knowing what each color means can be the difference between paying $500 for a book worth $500 and paying $500 for a book worth $50.

Complete CGC Label Guide

🔵

CGC Universal

Unrestored, no unwitnessed signatures. The gold standard.

Market Value

Highest premiums - blue label books command top market prices

Key Features
  • No restoration
  • No conservation
  • No signatures
  • Pure collectible form
🟣

CGC Restored

Color touch-ups, re-glued spine, trimming, added staples.

Market Value

70-90% less than blue label equivalent - collectors call it "PLOD" (Purple Label of Death)

Key Features
  • Restoration scale A-1 to C-5
  • A = Slight
  • C = Extensive
  • Quality rating included

Always check label color before buying key issues

🟢

CGC Qualified

Significant defect noted OR unwitnessed signature present.

Market Value

Discount vs blue label - varies widely based on specific qualification

Key Features
  • Missing coupon noted
  • Unwitnessed signature
  • Subscription crease
  • Tape presence

Grade reflects condition "as if" defect didn't exist

🟡

CGC Signature Series

Witnessed and verified signature by CGC representative.

Market Value

Can command significant premiums - especially Stan Lee, McFarlane, Jim Lee

Key Features
  • Authenticated signature
  • CGC witnessed
  • Guaranteed authentic
  • Creator verified

Special & Combo Labels

⬛🟡

CGC x JSA

Yellow + Black

Co-certification with James Spence Authentication. Signatures authenticated by both CGC and JSA — the gold standard for signed books.

🔵⚪

CGC Conserved

Blue + Grey

Minor stabilizing repairs for preservation (not enhancement). Removing damaging tape, professional cleaning. Valued closer to blue than purple.

Numeric Grade Quick Reference

GradeDescriptionMarket Status
10.0Gem Mint — Virtually perfectUltra-rare
9.8Near Mint/Mint — Most sought-afterPremium prices
9.6Near Mint+ — Exceptional copyStrong demand
8.0–9.4Very Fine rangeHighly collectible
6.0–7.5Fine range — Solid mid-gradeGood value
0.5Poor — But authenticatedKey issues only

The Bottom Line

Label color + numeric grade = the full picture of a book's value. Blue label 9.8s command the highest premiums. Purple labels trade at steep discounts. Green labels offer opportunities at the right price. Yellow labels add authentication value for signed books.

Pro Tip: Always verify label color before buying graded comics. The color tells you as much about value as the number.
